subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Master’s Degree in Computer Science
Completed undergraduate studies in Computer Engineering
Attended coursework covering algorithms, software engineering, and systems design
Participated in university research projects involving programming automation
Completed capstone project on optimizing CNC toolpaths using AI

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Job title: Computer Numerically Controlled (CNC) Tool Programmer
Role focuses on programming both front-end and back-end components of CNC systems
Designs and implements computer systems used for automated manufacturing tasks
Regularly reviews and modifies source code for CNC software
Collaborates with engineering teams to optimize machine tool performance

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the advanced manufacturing technology sector
Focuses on software solutions for automation in manufacturing
Operates at the intersection of computer science and industrial engineering
Supports digital transformation in manufacturing processes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Employed by a mid-sized technology company (100-500 employees)
Company emphasizes innovation and adoption of new technologies
Work environment is collaborative and engineering-driven
Remote and hybrid work options available to technical staff

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Serves clients in manufacturing automation and industrial software
Develops custom CNC programming solutions for machine shops and factories
Market segment includes aerospace, automotive, and precision engineering sectors
Company offers both software products and consulting services

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has 1-2 years of experience in the current CNC Tool Programmer role
Entered the workforce directly after completing Master’s degree
Has participated in several full project cycles since joining the company

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Holds a junior to early-career professional position
Typically reports to a lead developer or engineering manager
Involved in technical decision-making but not in managerial responsibilities
Participates in mentorship and onboarding sessions for new hires

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Programs and debugs CNC machine control software
Develops both front-end user interfaces and back-end logic for CNC systems
Designs new computer systems and optimizes existing architectures
Reviews and interprets source code, often through source dives
Uses LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT) daily to understand, document, and troubleshoot code

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Approximately 50% of time spent on writing and reviewing code
Around 25% dedicated to system design and architecture discussions
Roughly 15% on debugging and troubleshooting tasks
10% spent collaborating with peers or consulting documentation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Has moderate autonomy in choosing programming approaches and tools
Makes independent decisions on code structure and tool selection for daily tasks
Major architectural or deployment decisions require team lead approval
Can propose process improvements but final implementation is team-based

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Quickly comprehends and navigates source code repositories
Rapidly implements standard back-end features and APIs
Comfortable using LLMs to generate code explanations or summaries
Effortlessly adapts to new programming frameworks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Debugging legacy code with minimal documentation
Integrating multiple software systems with differing architectures
Optimizing CNC toolpaths for highly specialized manufacturing needs
Diagnosing rare and intermittent system failures

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Routine code documentation and commenting
Standardized code reviews for style and formatting
Generating initial boilerplate code for new modules
Batch testing of code changes across multiple configurations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Finds system design and creative problem solving most engaging
Enjoys learning new programming languages or tools for specific projects
Considers manual data entry and repetitive code formatting tedious
Prefers tasks that require critical thinking over highly repetitive ones

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Time-consuming to understand poorly documented codebases
Integration issues when tools lack interoperability
Manual testing and verification processes can be slow
Occasional LLM-generated code lacks context or introduces subtle bugs

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Productivity increases when working on engaging, novel problems
Skill level is higher in tasks that are also personally enjoyable
Tedious or repetitive tasks tend to decrease motivation and output
Enjoys collaborating with peers, which enhances both skill and productivity

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Very positive and motivated to stay up-to-date with emerging tech
Frequently explores new software recommended by the developer community
Considers tech learning essential for career advancement
Enjoys experimenting with beta versions of new programming tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to new tools, often self-teaching through online resources
Comfortable switching between programming languages and development environments
Can learn new LLM-based platforms with minimal onboarding
Views adaptability as a key strength in a technology-driven role

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Regularly allocates personal time for online courses or tutorials
Participates in internal training sessions when offered
Prioritizes learning when new tools directly benefit workflow
Balances training with work deadlines to ensure productivity

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by the prospect of increased efficiency and capability
Seeks out tech with direct application to current projects
Occasionally limited by high workload or tight project timelines
Less inclined to learn tools that lack clear integration with existing systems

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers often share recommendations and experiences with new tools
Management encourages experimentation with AI and automation platforms
Team-based adoption of tech increases willingness to invest in learning
Company culture values early adoption of proven technologies

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Cursor as a primary code editor and IDE
Engages ChatGPT for code assistance and explanations
Employs Perplexity for rapid information retrieval and research
Works with version control systems like Git for code management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Relies on Git and GitHub for source control and collaboration
Uses Docker for environment management and deployment
Utilizes Jira or similar tools for task tracking and project management
Employs debugging tools integrated into the code editor

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Proficient in Python, C++, and JavaScript for CNC programming
Familiar with RESTful APIs and web-based control interfaces
Has experience with SQL and NoSQL databases for system back-ends
Keeps updated on current best practices in software engineering

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Occasionally encounters compatibility issues between AI assistants and local codebases
Some code editors require manual configuration for LLM integration
Data exchange between older CNC systems and modern software can be challenging
Version mismatches between project dependencies can disrupt workflow

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic machine learning concepts such as supervised learning and model inference
Familiar with the architecture of large language models (LLMs)
Able to explain terms like prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and model hallucination
Has completed coursework or online modules related to AI fundamentals

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses ChatGPT daily for code understanding and explanation
Frequently employs Perplexity for technical research
Integrates Cursor’s AI features for code completion and suggestions
Experiments with other open-source LLMs for specialized tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Applies ChatGPT every day to describe and summarize unfamiliar code
Uses Perplexity multiple times a day for sourcing technical documentation
Employs AI-driven code completion to speed up routine development tasks
Occasionally leverages LLMs for brainstorming and generating test cases

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: AI has successfully helped decode legacy code significantly faster than manual reading
Experienced hallucinated explanations from LLMs, requiring manual verification
LLM-generated code snippets have occasionally introduced subtle logic errors
Learned the value of combining AI suggestions with personal code reviews

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: Company provides informal peer-led workshops on LLM applications
Access to internal documentation on integrating AI tools into workflow
Peers frequently share tips and best practices for AI use in programming
Formal AI training sessions are limited but encouraged by management

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers step-by-step collaboration for complex or unfamiliar code
Uses independent mode for routine code generation or documentation
Switches between modes depending on task difficulty and urgency
Finds step-by-step prompting enhances understanding and reduces errors

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Treats AI tools primarily as intelligent assistants for code-related tasks
Relies on AI as an advisor when exploring new frameworks or libraries
Occasionally uses AI as a co-worker during brainstorming sessions
Maintains final control and responsibility for the delivered code

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Highly open to experimenting with new AI-powered processes
Quickly adopts AI-driven workflows if they demonstrate clear benefits
Encourages peers to try out new AI integrations
Evaluates AI-driven changes critically for long-term viability

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces for brainstorming and code exploration
Uses command-based prompts for precise, repeatable tasks
Finds conversational UIs more intuitive for learning and debugging
Switches to command-based interactions when speed is a priority

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Many tasks require deep understanding of CNC programming standards
Tacit knowledge of machine tool behavior and real-world constraints is often necessary
LLMs can assist with general code but not specific manufacturing nuances
Leverages personal experience to validate AI-generated solutions

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Moderate trust in AI-generated code for non-critical or experimental tasks
High scrutiny of AI outputs for safety-critical manufacturing software
Consistently reviews and tests AI-suggested changes before deployment
Trust increases when AI explanations are transparent and well-documented

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI to handle repetitive coding and documentation tasks
Human oversight is essential for system design and integration decisions
Combines AI-generated drafts with manual refinement for best results
Balances automation with personal accountability for deliverables

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for code formatting, syntax checks, and boilerplate generation
Cautious about automating safety-critical logic or machine control functions
Views excessive automation without human review as potentially risky
Supports automation if override and manual correction options are available

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Believes AI enhances efficiency but does not replace core programming skills
Views communication and problem-solving as increasingly important alongside AI tools
Relies on fundamental coding skills to validate and improve AI outputs
Considers domain expertise even more critical for interpreting AI recommendations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Prompt engineering and effective use of LLMs are becoming key skills
Develops skills in evaluating and integrating AI-generated code
Responsible for maintaining documentation on AI-assisted workflows
Involved in educating peers about safe and effective AI adoption

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Mild concern about automation of repetitive programming tasks
Confident that system design and complex debugging will remain human-led
Sees AI as a tool to augment rather than replace CNC programmers
Monitors industry trends for shifts in job requirements

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Manual code review is standard before deploying AI-generated content
Workflow includes checkpoints to validate AI-suggested changes
Can revert or edit any automated changes before final implementation
Company encourages documenting exceptions and overrides for learning

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Company has become more open to AI tool experimentation in recent years
Policies now permit use of LLMs for non-confidential code review
Encouraged to share feedback on AI tool effectiveness and risks
Team discussions increasingly consider ethical and security implications of AI

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI will significantly accelerate innovation in manufacturing
Expects AI to democratize access to advanced programming skills
Acknowledges potential societal disruption from rapid automation
Sees AI as a catalyst for new job roles in tech and engineering

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Supports transparent AI systems with clear documentation of logic
Cautious about biases and inaccuracies in LLM outputs
Advocates for human oversight in safety-critical environments
Believes ethical AI adoption requires ongoing training and education

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Desires AI that can fully understand and map complex codebases
Wants LLMs to provide context-aware debugging and optimization suggestions
Seeks seamless integration of AI with all major programming environments
Would benefit from AI-driven real-time code quality and risk assessment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Anticipates spending more time on system design and less on routine coding
Expects AI to handle most code documentation and initial reviews
Foresees increased need for AI oversight and validation roles
Believes continuous learning will be essential as AI tools evolve

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Invest in regular training on AI tools and best practices
Develop guidelines for safe and ethical AI use in code development
Encourage cross-team sharing of AI tool adoption experiences
Establish clear processes for validating and overriding AI-generated outputs
